title: Fractional Controller
description:
**FRACTIONAL CONTROLLER**
CFO Hub, now part of CRI, provides outsourced CFO, controller, and accounting services for growing enterprises to augment and streamline their operations. By partnering with the CFO Hub team, our clients have access to financial professionals with decades of experience without the need for a full\-time staff.
We are seeking a Controller level individual to help manage the accounting functions for various clients. This position is initially a part\-time position with an opportunity for full\-time employment in the future. The ideal controller candidate has experience in technical accounting and financial statement. Public accounting experience is a plus.
We are dedicated to uniting your professional passions with your individual personal pursuits, creating an empowered workplace for everyone—from interns and admins to staff accountants and future partners. At CRI, \#EverybodyCounts.
**Essential Functions:**
* Own the monthly close process and preparation of accurate, GAAP\-compliant financial statements
* Manage day\-to\-day accounting operations, including AP/AR, payroll coordination, and reconciliations
* Maintain and strengthen internal controls, accounting policies, and compliance processes
* Support accounting system implementation, optimization, and documentation of procedures

**Qualifications:**
* 3\+ years of Controller experience
	+ Ability to manage month\-end close, deadlines, and reporting timelines
	+ Experience supporting organizations across multiple industries or complex business models
	+ Experience preparing or supporting financial statements, reconciliations, and audit requests
	+ Solid understanding of GAAP, including revenue recognition and accrual\-based accounting
* Hands\-on experience with accounting and ERP systems (QuickBooks Online, Sage Intacct, Xero, or similar)
* Bachelor’s degree in Accounting, Finance, or a related field required
	+ CPA license or active progress toward certification preferred, but not required

***Applicants must be authorized to work in the U.S. without the need for employment\-based visa sponsorship now or in the future. CRI will not sponsor applicants for U.S. work visa status for this position or future positions (no sponsorship is available for H\-1B, L\-1, TN, O\-1, E\-3, H\-1B1, F\-1, J\-1, OPT, CPT or any other employment\-based visa).***
CRI is a nationally recognized accounting and advisory firm known for its audit, tax, and business consulting expertise. The profession and our communities widely acknowledge our commitment to delivering tailored solutions to help ensure client success.
We stand out through our collaborative, client\-focused approach, offering personalized services backed by deep industry knowledge. Clients trust CRI as a dedicated partner who understands their unique needs and ensures support at every step. To learn more about the industries we serve, our service offerings, and our family of companies please visit us at www.criadv.com.
CRI offers a generous benefits package that includes 21 PTO days, 9 paid holidays, and company\-paid long\-term disability and life insurance. Employees also have access to Blue Cross Blue Shield medical insurance, as well as dental, vision, and short\-term disability coverage. Additional benefits include parental leave, flexible spending accounts, and a 401(k) retirement plan.
*The anticipated hiring base hourly rate for this role is: $70\-$75*
*Actual compensation is based on several factors that are unique to each candidate, including but not limited to education, skill set, depth of experience, certifications, and specific work location.*

title: Accounting Manager
description:
**Accounting Manager**

Coordinate a team of accountants to provide timely and accurate financial reports, ultimately delivering world\-class accounting and advisory solutions for nonprofit organizations.

**Job Title:** Accounting Manager

**Classification:** Full\-time, exempt

**Workplace Type:** Hybrid (3 days in\-office required)

**Department:** Client Services

**Reporting To:** CFO

As an **Accounting Manager** for our rapidly growing company, you will assist in the day\-to\-day management of a team of 7\-8 professionals, consisting of 4\-5 Accounting Associates and Senior Accounting Associates, 1 Accounting Assistant, and up to 1 Accounting Intern. You will report to the CFO, who serves as the leader of your entire team. Our CFO teams are responsible for ensuring the accurate and timely delivery of a full suite of accounting and bookkeeping services to a wide range of nonprofit clients. We are looking for an intrinsically motivated problem\-solver who values transparency, inclusion, collaboration, and impact. At The Charity CFO, you will leverage your passion for accounting and the nonprofit sector to make a lasting impact for your clients.

In this role, you are expected to work closely with your team to ensure that all client deliverables are accurate and on time. You will rarely be expected to meet directly with clients; you will work primarily behind the scenes to understand your clients’ unique needs and accounting processes, and you will be responsible for ensuring that your team follows those processes to fidelity.

**Who is The Charity CFO, LLC?**

We provide premier accounting, advisory, and fractional CFO solutions for nonprofits. Our mission is to help nonprofit leaders by creating clarity and understanding of their organization’s financial position, modernizing financial operations, and bringing the focus back to what really matters \- the people and communities they serve. Our vision is to be the largest nonprofit accounting service firm in the United States.

**Our Culture \& Values**

At The Charity CFO, we put our people before revenue or clients, which has allowed us to create a unique workplace that values you first as a human being and then as an employee. We’ll provide you with all the support and training you need to crush your goals inside the workplace, along with the flexibility, benefits, and downtime to build a life you love outside the office.

Inside our doors, you’ll find a respectful, collaborative environment with team members with all levels of professional experience from beautifully diverse backgrounds. Everyone is welcome here, and you’ll be judged solely on your performance, regardless of any limitations society may have previously placed on you.

**Our Benefits**

From career advancement to free parking to 14 paid holidays, 4 weeks of PTO, and paid new parental leave, our comprehensive benefits package makes it easier to be there for your family, plan for the future and truly enjoy your downtime. Learn more about all of these benefits .

**What You Would Be Helping Us With:**

The Accounting Manager has many responsibilities. They assist their CFO in the day\-to\-day operations of a team of 7\-8 professionals, and they are responsible for serving 30\-40 clients. This requires that they have advanced accounting knowledge, comfortability with technology, and some management experience. Accounting Manager responsibilities can be divided into two major categories: management responsibilities and client responsibilities.

Management Responsibilities:

* Lead staff meetings and training.
* Answer team member questions.
* Proactively track team\-wide tasks, projects, and upcoming deadlines to remove roadblocks and ensure that your team has the tools they need to complete each task correctly and on time.
* Leverage Accounting Assistants and Interns to facilitate the delegation of team tasks whenever possible such that every member of the team is fully utilized.
* Plan for team member PTO or other absences to ensure that client deliverables are still met.
* Assist in the onboarding of new employees to ensure their successful transition to the company.
* Work closely with the Quality Assurance Team to ensure that client accounting systems are accurate, audits are properly managed, and all compliance deadlines are met.
* Facilitate training workshops for new hires.
* Seek out opportunities for professional development and ongoing education.
* Fill\-in for team member absences, as necessary.

Client Responsibilities:

* Review client financial reports, as prepared by Accounting Associates and Senior Accounting Associates, on a monthly basis to ensure accuracy and compliance with GAAP.
* Ensure that the team is proactive in tracking spend\-down of clients’ restricted grants. Review all new client grant agreements monthly to ensure that grants are accounted for and tracked properly and to identify any compliance expectations that may impact our accounting processes.
* Review client financial statements to ensure that they are accurate and ‘audit\-ready’ prior to the commencement of financial statement audits.
* Monitor ongoing client audits to ensure that the timeline remains on track.
* Review client audit drafts at the conclusion of audit fieldwork to ensure accuracy.
* Conduct an end\-of\-year review of client financial statements prior to the preparation of the annual Form 990 return. The actual preparation and review of the Form 990 will not fall under the responsibility of the Accounting Manager.
* Review all other client deliverables prepared by the team to ensure accuracy.
* Facilitate the annual 1099 filing process for all assigned clients.
* Participate in new client onboarding meetings to understand their unique needs and to build customized accounting processes.
* Conduct an internal ‘desk review’ of 3\-4 clients per month to identify areas for improvement and to develop a game plan for cleaning\-up any accounting issues.
* Ensure that your team maintains appropriate accounting processes for all assigned clients. Proactively revise accounting processes as necessary.
* Tackle client\-related projects, such as building workpapers, investigating accounting discrepancies, completing chart of account revisions, performing cash\- to accrual\-basis conversions, etc.

**Qualifications:**

* Bachelor’s in Accounting or Accounting\-related field
* 3\-5 years of experience in full\-cycle accounting or public accounting as an auditor or outsourced accountant
* 1\-2 years of nonprofit accounting experience, preferred
* 1\-2 years of management experience, preferred
* CPA license, preferred
* Proficiency with Microsoft Excel
* Proficiency with accounting software
* Excellent critical thinking skills
* Advanced accounting knowledge

**What Success Looks Like in the First 90 Days:**

Like we mentioned above, we’ll provide you with all the support and training you need to crush your goals inside the workplace. For this role, this means that within 90 days, we will prepare you to:

* Review financial statements to identify errors and provide feedback to team members.
* Ensure that accounting systems are properly maintained and that processes and workpapers adhere to the firm’s standards.
* Manage your team to ensure client needs are met and deliverables are consistently on time.
* Maintain accounting processes to ensure quality and to meet our clients’ unique needs
* Facilitate our clients’ audit process to minimize the chance of audit adjustments or findings.
* Be an ambassador for The Charity CFO, externally and internally.

**How to Apply**

We want to acknowledge that imposter syndrome is real, so bullet points aside, if our mission, culture \& values resonate with you, we want to hear from you! If you are someone who is committed to connecting the dots, supporting clients and colleagues, and want to work in a position that allows you to develop your accounting experience on work that really matters, please apply on our .

If you require special accommodations during the recruitment process, please email our recruiting team whose contact information can be found at the bottom of our .

*The Charity CFO, LLC is an equal\-opportunity employer. We do not discriminate based on race, religion, color, national origin, dietary preferences, gender, age, fashion sense, marital status, veteran status, disability status, political leanings, or any other category protected by applicable federal, state or local laws.*

**Additional Notices**

*Employment at The Charity CFO is at\-will. This job description is not an exhaustive list of all functions that may be required, and The Charity CFO reserves the right to require additional functions as well as to revise this job description at any time.*

title: Client Accounting Advisory Services (CAAS) Manager
description:
**Client Accounting Advisory Services (CAAS) Manager**

Position Summary

The CAAS Manager is a hybrid leadership and high\-level consulting role. This individual is responsible for the dual mandate of overseeing a dedicated bookkeeping team and delivering high\-impact fractional Controller and accounting services directly to a diverse client portfolio. The ideal candidate will bridge the gap between technical execution and strategic financial advisory, particularly within the construction, manufacturing, and real estate sectors.

**Key Responsibilities**

Team Supervision \& Leadership

* Workflow Management: Oversee the daily operations of the bookkeeping team, ensuring accurate and timely completion of monthly, quarterly, and year\-end closings. Implement systems to track work in progress, completed deliverables, and outstanding items.
* Capacity Planning \& Workload Management: Develop and maintain a structured capacity model to track each bookkeeper’s client load, time allocation, and availability. Establish utilization targets and ensure balanced workload distribution across the team.
* Quality Assurance: Perform high\-level reviews of work papers, bank reconciliations, and financial statements to ensure compliance, accuracy, and consistency.
* Professional Development: Provide technical mentorship through regular check\-ins, coaching, and performance feedback to support team growth and maintain service standards.
* Technical Support Structure: Establish recurring office hours or structured support channels to address technical questions, standardize approaches, and reduce ad hoc interruptions.
* Technical Leadership \& Industry Awareness: Stay current with bookkeeping standards, QuickBooks updates, and regulatory changes. Communicate updates to team.
* New Hire Onboarding \& Training: Lead onboarding of new bookkeepers by developing and maintaining standardized training materials, including step\-by\-step procedures, preparation checklists, and process documentation. Ensure new hires are trained on workflows, expectations, and quality standards until they can independently manage client work.
* Training \& Certification: Ensure all bookkeeping staff complete required training, including QuickBooks Online certification within required timelines. Track progress and enforce accountability.
* Process Improvement \& System Design: Continuously improve bookkeeping processes to enhance productivity and client delivery.
* Client Intake Process: Manage a structured intake system for new client work from partners, ensuring all required information is captured before work begins.
* Work Delegation Framework: Assign work to bookkeepers based on capacity, experience, and skill level to ensure balanced workloads and effective execution.
* Onboarding Coordination (Clients): Oversee onboarding of new clients, ensuring proper setup, clear expectations, and a smooth transition to delivery team.
* Service Delivery Tracking: Implement systems to track completion of work and confirm delivery to clients, ensuring transparency into completed and in\-progress work. This can be easily seen by partners, and they can communicate with individual bookkeepers.

Client Advisory \& Fractional CFO Services

* Strategic Advisory: Serve as a fractional Controller for clients, providing cash flow forecasting, budget\-to\-actual analysis, and strategic financial planning.
* Industry\-Specific Accounting: Manage complex accounting needs for for\-profit clients, including construction (job costing, WIP), manufacturing (inventory valuation), and real estate (entity structuring, property distributions).
* Compliance \& Tax Liaison: Oversee payroll processing, state and local tax filings, and specialized compliance requirements.
* System Implementation: Lead migration or optimization of client accounting systems, ensuring seamless integration between QuickBooks and third\-party industry software.

**Required Qualifications \& Skills**

Technical Expertise:

* Advanced Proficiency: QuickBooks Online (QBO) and QuickBooks Desktop (QBD).
* Payroll \& Tax: Comprehensive experience with major payroll platforms and navigating multi\-state tax compliance.
* Industry Knowledge: Strong background in accounting, payroll, and state compliance and tax law.

Experience: 8\+ years of progressive accounting experience, with at least 1–2 years in a supervisory or lead capacity.

Education: Bachelor’s degree in Accounting or Finance; CPA or CMA designation is preferred but not required.

Soft Skills: Exceptional communication skills for client\-facing advisory and the ability to pivot between granular data and high\-level strategy.

Work Environment \& Systems

The CAAS Manager must be comfortable operating in a tech\-forward environment, utilizing a variety of accounting platforms and client\-specific tools. This role requires strong organizational skills and the ability to manage multiple deadlines while building scalable systems to support growth.

Pay: $100,000\.00 \- $150,000\.00 per year

Benefits:

* 401(k)
* 401(k) matching
* Dental insurance
* Disability insurance
* Health insurance
* Life insurance
* Paid time off
* Vision insurance

Work Location: In person

title: Controller
description:
**Controller**

*Client Advisory \& Team Leadership Focus*

**Location:** Hybrid – North Loop, Minneapolis, MN (Twin Cities Metro Area)

**Summary**

Join **Owl Bookkeeping and CFO Services** as a **hands\-on Controller** who thrives on variety, challenge, and impact. Partner with entrepreneurs, lead accounting excellence, and make a meaningful difference for growing businesses.

**About the Role**

Are you a seasoned **Controller** who thrives in a **collaborative, challenging, and rewarding environment**—where every day brings new opportunities to solve problems, support business owners, and make an impact?

At **Owl Bookkeeping and CFO Services**, we’re looking for a **hands\-on Controller** to deliver high\-level financial insight and accounting leadership across multiple client engagements. In some partnerships, you’ll collaborate with a bookkeeper who supports day\-to\-day transactions; in others, you’ll handle the full controller scope yourself.

This position is ideal for someone who enjoys **diving into complex financials, improving processes, and providing trusted strategic guidance** to business owners. You’ll work directly with entrepreneurs to deliver clear, accurate financials and actionable insights that drive growth.

This role is perfect for a **self\-starter** who enjoys variety, autonomy, and meaningful client relationships.

**What You’ll Do**

* Lead **accounting processes** for multiple clients, ensuring **accuracy, timeliness, and insight.**
* Collaborate with accounting staff to deliver quality financials and process improvements.
* Prepare **budgets, forecasts, and performance analyses.**
* Advise clients with **strategic, data\-driven recommendations.**
* Support **documentation, workflow improvements, and system optimization.**

**What You Bring**

* Proven experience providing **controller\-level financial oversight.**
* Strong **QuickBooks Online and Desktop proficiency.**
* Ability to manage **multiple clients and priorities** with precision.
* Excellent **communication** and **relationship\-building** skills.
* Commitment to **accuracy, accountability, and continuous improvement.**
* Experience mentoring or collaborating with **bookkeepers or accounting staff.**
* **Knowledge of GAAP** and financial reporting best practices.

**Why You’ll Love Working at Owl**

* **Hybrid flexibility** – work from our North Loop office and onsite with local clients.
* **Supportive, growth\-oriented team culture.**
* **Career development opportunities** in client strategy and leadership.
* **Paid parking, free breakfast and snacks, onsite gym, 401(k) match, tiered PTO, and generous benefits.**

If you’re looking for a place where your expertise matters — where you can **mentor others, advise clients, and shape the financial health of growing businesses** — apply today!

* 401(k) matching

title: Fractional Chief Financial Officer (CFO)
description:
Our team provides superior services, solutions and subject matter expertise to solve our customer's most challenging requirements in cyber, land, air, sea and space. We believe in empowering our employees to deliver exceptional results across our federal, state and private sector clients. We are values\-led and mission driven. We can't wait to work with you!

 **About the role**


We are seeking an experienced **Fractional Chief Financial Officer (CFO)** to provide strategic financial leadership to a growing government contracting firm. This role is ideal for a seasoned finance executive with deep expertise in **GovCon financial management, compliance, and operational scaling**.

  

The Fractional CFO will serve as a trusted advisor to executive leadership, ensuring financial integrity, supporting strategic decision\-making, and maintaining compliance with government contracting requirements. This role requires **regular onsite presence in Bethesda, MD** and direct engagement with clients and stakeholders.

 **What you'll do**

* Lead financial strategy, planning, and forecasting to support organizational growth and profitability
* Develop and maintain **robust financial models** to support business decisions, contract pricing, and scenario planning
* Ensure compliance with **government contracting regulations**, including FAR, CAS (as applicable), and audit readiness
* Oversee financial operations for a **multi\-state employer**, including revenue recognition, indirect rate management, and cost accounting structures
* Partner with executive leadership on **budgeting, pipeline analysis, and strategic investments**
* Support **client\-facing financial discussions**, including pricing, cost structures, and financial performance
* Maintain and enhance financial processes aligned with **ISO 9001 standards** and internal quality controls
* Supervise and mentor a small finance team (1–2 professionals), ensuring accuracy, efficiency, and professional development
* Provide insights and recommendations to improve financial performance, cash flow, and operational efficiency

**Qualifications**

* Proven experience as a CFO, Fractional CFO, or senior finance leader within a **government contracting environment**
* Strong knowledge of **GovCon compliance**, including FAR, indirect rates, DCAA audits, and contract financial management
* Demonstrated expertise in **financial modeling, forecasting, and strategic planning**
* Experience managing finances for a **small to mid\-sized government contractor**
* Familiarity with **ISO 9001 standards** and quality management systems
* Experience supporting **multi\-state payroll and compliance environments**
* Strong leadership experience, including supervision of small finance/accounting teams
* Excellent communication skills with the ability to engage effectively in **client\-facing settings**
* CPA, MBA, or equivalent advanced credential preferred

 **What We're Looking For**

* A strategic thinker who can operate both **hands\-on and at the executive level**
* A collaborative partner who can translate financial data into actionable business insights
* A leader comfortable navigating the complexities of **growth\-stage government contractors**
* A professional who thrives in a **dynamic, client\-facing environment**
* Fractional / part\-time structure with flexibility based on business needs
* Regular onsite presence required in **Bethesda, MD**
* Opportunity to make a meaningful impact on organizational growth and financial maturity

  

The pay range for this position is between $5k to $15k per month based on experience and final work schedule. For positions averaging 30 hours per week or more, IQ Sig benefits are available including; Health, Dental, Vision, 401k w/ match, Gym Membership discounts, Volunteer Paid Time Off, and a generous PTO package. We'd love to tell you more! Apply today!

  

We believe great talent comes from many backgrounds and experiences. We are proud to be an Equal Opportunity Employer and make all employment decisions based on merit, qualifications, and business needs. We are committed to maintaining a workplace free from discrimination and harassment, consistent with all applicable laws.

title: Director of Accounting Services
description:
**Director, Accounting Services**

**Location:**

Remote — with occasional travel for company all\-hands, select conferences, and key client/vendor meetings

**Reports To:**

Andy Gauthier, Founder \& CEO (near\-term); VP of Service Delivery (as org scales)

**Department:**

Accounting Services

**Type:**

Full\-Time

**Compensation:**

$120,000–$135,000 base \+ performance bonus up to 20% \+ clear path to VP

**Apply here:** https://forms.gle/9ihy6K77ExAp8PFf6

**You will upload your resume and a short video introduction there.**

**Who We Are**

Mighty Financial partners with growth\-minded founders and operators — typically $2M–$20M in revenue — to bring clarity, discipline, and forward momentum to their financial operations. We deliver managed accounting and advisory services that help clients make better decisions and build stronger companies.

We’ve been around for six years, had another record year in 2025 (33% YoY Growth), and are on track for 45% growth in 2026 with intention. We are industry agnostic. Our clients include venture\-backed startups, services firms, light manufacturing, real estate development, and more. Almost everything we’ve built has come through referrals. This is a testament to the quality of our work and the relationships we keep.

We are operationally sound, people\-first, and relentlessly improving. If you want to own something that matters, this is the place.

**The Role**

This is a player\-coach position. Roughly 80% of your time will be hands\-on with clients — owning the work, not just overseeing it. The other 20% is leadership: developing your team, protecting margins, and helping Mighty scale with quality intact.

You’ll lead a portfolio of clients and a team of Accounting Managers and Accountants. You’re the one accountable for accurate, timely reporting, strong client relationships, and engagement profitability. This isn’t a caretaking role — it’s a leadership role with real P\&L visibility and direct impact on the firm’s growth.

This role is a direct path to VP of Service Delivery as Mighty scales. We’re building the leadership bench now.

**Core Responsibilities1\. Portfolio Ownership**

* Own a defined client portfolio — quality, accuracy, relationships, and margins
* Ensure month\-end close deadlines are met consistently (target: ≥95% on time)
* Review financial statements for accuracy, clarity, and completeness
* Surface insights beyond compliance — identify what the numbers mean for the client’s business

**2\. Team Leadership**

* Lead, coach, and develop Accounting Managers and Accountants
* Own workload allocation and capacity planning for your team
* Conduct performance reviews and create meaningful development plans
* Hold the standard — your team’s output reflects your leadership

**3\. Technical Excellence**

* Ensure GAAP\-compliant reporting across applicable client engagements
* Oversee reconciliations, accruals, and revenue recognition with precision
* Standardize processes across accounts — reduce variability, increase repeatability
* Maintain deep QBO expertise and help team members build theirs

**4\. Advisory Mindset**

* Identify value\-add advisory opportunities within your portfolio
* Collaborate with Fractional CFOs and strategic consultants
* Translate numbers into clear, actionable insights founders can use
* Build client relationships that go beyond the monthly close

**5\. Operational Discipline**

* Maintain SOP compliance and documentation standards
* Track engagement profitability — know your margins
* Keep work in scope; when it isn’t, escalate or re\-engage proactively

**Director Scorecard**

We manage by outcomes, not activity. Here’s how success is measured:

**Area**

**Metrics**

**Operational**

* ≥ 95% on\-time month\-end close within portfolio
* Error rate at or below defined QC threshold
* Timely, complete reconciliations every cycle

**Financial**

* Portfolio gross margin ≥ target
* Realization rate ≥ target
* Scope adherence maintained; overages flagged proactively

**Client**

* Portfolio retention ≥ target
* Expansion opportunities identified and surfaced quarterly
* Clients experience reliable, clear communication

**People**

* Direct reports meeting performance standards
* Team utilization within target range
* Documented development milestones completed

**What Success Looks Like in 12 Months**

* Your portfolio runs cleanly and predictably — clients rarely have surprises
* Your team members are more skilled and confident than when you arrived
* Advisory conversations with clients increase naturally because they trust your judgment
* Engagements stay profitable — you know your margins and you protect them
* You’ve helped Mighty take a meaningful step toward building a scalable delivery system

**Qualifications**

**REQUIRED**

* 7\+ years of progressive accounting experience, with at least 2 in a client\-facing or multi\-client environment
* 2\+ years leading and developing a team of accountants
* Advanced proficiency in QuickBooks Online (QBO)
* Strong financial statement review and quality control skills
* Excellent written and verbal communication — you can explain complex financials to non\-financial founders clearly
* Proven ability to manage multiple clients and competing deadlines without dropping balls

**STRONGLY PREFERRED**

* CPA (active or inactive) — or actively pursuing licensure
* Experience in an outsourced accounting, fractional CFO, or multi\-client services environment
* Familiarity with startup or venture\-backed company financials (ASC 606, board reporting, audit prep)

**Compensation \& Benefits**

We pay for ownership and performance:

**Base Salary**

$120,000 – $135,000 (based on experience)

**Performance Bonus**

Up to 20% of base, tied to portfolio profitability, close performance, and client retention

**Retirement**

401(k) with 4% company match

**PTO**

Unlimited

**Long\-Term Upside**

Incentive potential tied to firm performance as we scale

**Remote Setup**

$500 work\-from\-home stipend

**Development**

Up to $3,000/year for CPE, certifications, and professional development

**Career Path**

Clear path to VP, Service Delivery — we’re building that seat for the right person

**How to Apply**

We review candidates based on who they actually are — not just what their resume says. Here’s what we ask of everyone who applies:

**Step 1: Submit Your Resume**

Send us your resume with a brief note on why this role caught your attention.

**Step 2: Record a 60–120 Second Video Introduction**

This is required. We want to hear from you before we talk to you. In your video, please answer both of the following questions:

* **Tell us about yourself — but skip the resume walkthrough.** What drives you? What kind of work makes you feel like you’re firing on all cylinders? What’s something you’re genuinely proud of that a hiring manager might never ask about?
* **You’re managing three clients, two deadlines collide, and one of your accountants needs support on a technical issue — all at once.** Walk us through how you think about it. We’re not looking for the “right” answer — we want to see how you operate under real pressure.

Keep it to 60–120 seconds. Shoot it on your phone if you need to — production quality doesn’t matter. Clarity, confidence, and candor do.

title: Fractional Chief Financial Officer (Project-Based)
description:
### **Description**

  

We are a high\-performing, fast\-growing personal injury law firm located in the heart of Newport Beach, dedicated to fighting for justice on behalf of injured clients. Our firm is known for exceptional results, a client\-centered approach, and a strong culture of professionalism and accountability.  

  

We are seeking an experienced Fractional CFO to provide strategic financial leadership and project\-based support for a growing personal injury law firm in California. This role is ideal for a finance executive with deep expertise in legal industry accounting, contingency fee structures, and trust accounting compliance.  

  

The Fractional CFO will partner closely with firm leadership to optimize financial performance, improve cash flow management, and implement scalable financial systems.### **Responsibilities:**

 **Strategic Financial Leadership*** Provide high\-level financial strategy aligned with firm growth goals
* Advise partners on profitability, expansion planning, and capital allocation
* Develop financial models for case pipeline forecasting and revenue timing

**Financial Planning \& Analysis (FP\&A)*** Build and maintain budgets, forecasts, and KPI dashboards
* Analyze case costs, settlement timelines, and fee realization rates
* Deliver actionable insights on profitability by case type, attorney, and referral source

**Cash Flow \& Contingency Fee Management*** Manage and forecast irregular cash flows tied to settlements and verdicts
* Optimize working capital and liquidity planning
* Advise on funding strategies (e.g., lines of credit, litigation financing)

**Trust Accounting \& Compliance*** Ensure compliance with California Bar trust accounting (IOLTA) requirements
* Oversee proper handling of client funds, settlements, and disbursements
* Coordinate with external auditors and legal compliance advisors

**Financial Systems \& Process Improvement*** Evaluate and improve accounting systems, reporting tools, and workflows
* Implement best practices for case cost tracking and financial reporting
* Integrate practice management and accounting platforms

  

### **Qualifications:**

  

* Bachelor's degree in accounting, finance, or a related field is required; CPA or MBA strongly preferred.
* Prior experience in a personal injury law firm setting, with working knowledge of marketing costs, operational expenditures, and revenue models specific to the industry is preferred.
* Proven experience in data\-driven performance analysis, including tracking and interpreting costs vs. returns across departments and individual staff in personal injury casework.
* Strong knowledge of legal accounting, trust/IOLTA compliance, and case\-based financial modeling.
* Proficient in legal practice management and accounting software (e.g., Clio, QuickBooks, NetDocuments).
* Excellent analytical, communication, and leadership skills.
* Strategic thinker with the ability to execute tactical plans.

  

### **Benefits:**

  

Contract or part\-time employees do not receive Firm benefits.### **About Avrek Law**


Avrek Law Firm is a trusted and highly regarded personal injury attorney. With a focus on achieving justice for their clients, they provide aggressive and thoughtful legal representation. If you have been injured in an accident caused by someone else's negligence, Avrek Law Firm is here to help you navigate through the legal process. Their experienced team understands the challenges faced by injured victims during this difficult time, as powerful private interests often stack the legal system against them. Avrek Law Firm levels the playing field by fighting for fair compensation for your injuries. They have a track record of success, having secured over $1 billion for their clients. Most importantly, they work on a contingency basis, meaning you won't pay anything until they win your case. Contact Avrek Law Firm today for a free consultation and let them fight for the justice you deserve.

title: Controller
description:
TONYMOLY USA is a leading K\-beauty brand located in Brooklyn, NY. We bring fun, effective, and innovative skincare to beauty lovers nationwide through our presence in major retailers, e\-commerce, and DTC channels. We're scaling and building a high\-performing team to support this next phase of growth.


We’re currently seeking an experienced and strategic **Controller** to oversee all accounting operations and lead financial reporting, planning, and controls. This is a hands\-on role for a highly analytical, operationally\-minded finance professional with deep experience in **distribution, inventory accounting**, and **ERP systems**.

### **Position Summary**


We are seeking a highly skilled and experienced **Controller** to lead the accounting function at TONYMOLY USA. The Controller will oversee all aspects of accounting operations, including the preparation of timely and accurate financial reports, the maintenance of robust accounting systems and internal controls, and the development of budgets and forecasts aligned with company goals.


This role requires a strategic thinker with a strong operational mindset \- someone who thrives in a fast\-paced, entrepreneurial environment and can build scalable processes without sacrificing precision. The ideal candidate will have deep experience in wholesale distribution and E\-commerce businesses, and must bring expert\-level proficiency in ERP systems. This is a hands\-on leadership role that will collaborate cross\-functionally across departments and help shape the financial infrastructure.

### **Key Responsibilities**

#### **Financial Management \& Reporting**

* Oversee all core accounting functions: billing, A/R, A/P, general ledger, cost accounting, inventory accounting, and revenue recognition
* Prepare timely and accurate monthly, quarterly, and annual financial statements in accordance with GAAP
* Maintain a robust system of accounting policies, internal controls, and procedures
* Manage intercompany transactions and reconciliations with TONYMOLY HQ and related entities
* Prepare management reporting packages and variance analyses for senior leadership
* Coordinate and support external audits, tax filings, and compliance reviews

**Accounts Receivable \& Collections**

* Manage the full AR cycle including invoicing, cash application, aging analysis, and collections for retail, wholesale, and e\-commerce customers (Target, Ulta Beauty, Amazon, Nordstrom Rack, and others)
* Monitor and dispute retailer chargebacks and deductions across all trading partners, including compliance deductions (VCNA, VC011, TR10, CB501\) through vendor portals such as Partners Online (Target) and Vendor Central (Amazon)
* Maintain aging reports and coordinate with sales and operations to resolve disputed balances

**Accounts Payable \& Cash Management**

* Oversee AP processing, vendor payment runs, and reconciliation of vendor statements
* Manage weekly cash flow forecasting and daily cash position reporting

#### **Inventory \& Distribution Focus**

* Manage inventory valuation, standard costing, and reconciliation across all channels (wholesale, eCommerce)
* Partner with operations and supply chain to ensure accurate and timely inventory tracking
* Identify and implement strategies to improve inventory turnover and reduce obsolescence
* Oversee freight, logistics, and landed cost accounting
* Identify and write off obsolete, non\-saleable, or slow\-moving inventory in coordination with operations

#### **ERP \& Financial Systems**

* Act as internal SAP Business One expert (FI/CO, MM, SD modules)
* Ensure data integrity and maximize operational efficiency through system optimization
* Lead enhancements, upgrades, and cross\-functional financial integrations

#### **Compliance \& Internal Controls**

* Ensure full compliance with federal, state, and local regulations
* Maintain strong internal controls over all financial transactions and reporting
* Act as primary liaison to external accounting partners

#### **Leadership**

* Promote a culture of ownership, accuracy, and continuous improvement
* Collaborate closely with executive leadership, Fractional CFO, sales, operations, and marketing

### **Qualifications**

* Bachelor’s degree in Accounting, Finance, or related field (CPA or MBA preferred)
* Minimum 7 years of progressive experience in accounting/finance, including 3\+ years in a Controller or senior finance leadership role
* Strong background in **wholesale/retail distribution**, inventory management, and COGS analysis
* **Expert\-level proficiency in SAP**
* Experience working with major U.S. retailers (e.g., Ulta, Target, Macy’s)
* Background in **beauty, skincare, or CPG** is highly desirable
* Exceptional analytical, leadership, and cross\-functional communication skills
* Thrives in a fast\-paced, entrepreneurial environment and can build structure within growth
* Experience managing retailer AR, chargebacks, and deduction disputes with major accounts (Target, Amazon, Ulta, Nordstrom, or similar)

### **Compensation \& Benefits**

* Competitive salary and performance\-based bonus
* Full health, dental, and vision insurance
* 401(k) retirement plan
* Generous employee discount on all beauty and skincare products
* Career development opportunities and leadership exposure within a growing company

title: Integrated Marketing Manager, Private Jet Services
description:
Job Title\- Integrated Marketing Manager, Private Jet Services


Department\- Marketing


Reports to\- Chief Marketing Officer


Location\- Plano, TX

**Who we are\-**


Founded in 1995, **Elevate Aviation Group** is the parent organization uniting Elevate Jet, Private Jet Services (PJS), and Elevate MRO under one standard of aviation excellence. We operate in the space between small operators and faceless corporations – large enough to deliver global capability, yet agile enough to provide true personal service. As we enter our fourth decade, we’re revolutionizing how we approach the market, combining decades of operational expertise with modern technology to deliver certainty, safety, and seamless experiences across every stage of private aviation – from charter to acquisition, management, and maintenance.

**Summary of position\-** We are looking for an Integrated Marketing Manager to own the full content and campaign marketing function for PJS. This is a senior individual contributor role reporting to the Chief Marketing Officer and serving as the primary marketing owner for PJS across all enterprise verticals — corporate, sports, entertainment, UHNW, government, and hospitality.


You will be responsible for bringing the PJS brand to life across every touchpoint: campaigns, one sheets, email, social, events, and vertical\-specific collateral. You are not a generalist executing tasks — you are a strategic content owner who understands enterprise client psychology, can translate complex service offerings into compelling narratives, and knows how to market a premium managed service to sophisticated buyers.

**Essential Duties \& Responsibilities\-**

**Campaign Marketing**

* Lead integrated campaign development across all PJS verticals from brief through execution
* Build campaign architecture that serves both long\-cycle enterprise sales and relationship\-driven retention
* Partner with paid media and PR agencies to ensure PJS messaging is consistent and on\-brand across all channels

**Content \& Collateral**

* Own all PJS one sheets, pitch decks, capabilities decks, and sales enablement materials
* Develop and maintain vertical\-specific content tailored to corporate travel managers, sports organizations, entertainment agencies, government procurement teams, and hospitality groups
* Ensure all PJS materials meet the brand standard: editorial quality, premium tone, zero ambiguity in value proposition

**Email Marketing**

* Manage PJS email marketing end\-to\-end in HubSpot — campaign emails, nurture sequences, client communications, and reactivation programs
* Develop segmented messaging strategies across the enterprise funnel: prospect, active client, lapsed client
* Partner with the Marketing Automation \& Intelligence Manager on list hygiene, segmentation logic, and performance reporting

**Social Media**

* Own PJS social presence across applicable platforms, with content strategy calibrated to an UHNW and enterprise B2B audience
* Develop an editorial calendar that supports campaign goals, vertical activation, and brand positioning
* Distinguish PJS tone and content approach from Elevate Jet without creating audience confusion

**Events**

* Support planning and execution of PJS client events, industry activations, and partnership\-driven experiences
* Develop event marketing materials, pre\- and post\-event communications, and on\-site collateral
* Coordinate with the CMO and events function on activations tied to strategic partnerships (e.g., TIGER 21, sports industry)

**Knowledge, Skills \& Abilities**

**Required**

* 5–8 years of marketing experience with at least 2–3 years in private aviation or business aviation.
* Demonstrated experience owning content and campaign marketing for a managed service, charter operation, or high\-touch B2B product
* Deep understanding of enterprise buyer behavior across corporate, sports, entertainment, or government verticals
* Exceptional writing ability — you can draft a capabilities deck, a client email, and a social caption with equal command
* Proficiency in HubSpot (campaign execution, segmentation, reporting) or a comparable CRM/MAP
* Strong project management skills with the ability to manage multiple campaigns and stakeholders simultaneously
* Experience working with creative, PR, and paid media agency partners

**Preferred**

* Background in managed charter, fractional ownership, or jet card program marketing
* Familiarity with UHNW audience marketing, wealth\-adjacent media, or luxury lifestyle brand positioning
* Experience supporting a sales organization with vertical\-specific collateral and enablement materials
* Exposure to partnership or co\-marketing programs within sports, entertainment, or hospitality ecosystems
* 4–7 years of experience in marketing, brand, or content\-focused roles
* Aviation or aerospace industry experience preferred, particularly within private aviation, travel, or high\-consideration services

**Schedule \& Travel**

* Standard business hours are expected for this role, however there may be times when nights or weekends may be required to meet business needs
* Travel may be required up to 10%


Miscellaneous

* FLSA Status – Full time, exempt
* Lifting requirements – Up to 20 lbs
* U.S. work authorization is a precondition of employment. Applicants must be authorized to work for any employer in the U.S., both now and at all future times. The company is unable to sponsor or assume sponsorship of employment visas.

title: Fractional Chief Medical Officer (CMO) – Primary Care (Value-Based Care Focus)
description:

Rio Grande Valley, TX (Hybrid with periodic on\-site presence in McAllen, Edinburg, and Pharr)

**Full Job Description**

ADIRA Health is seeking a high\-impact, execution\-driven Fractional Chief Medical Officer (CMO) to lead and scale our clinical platform across a growing network of primary care clinics.

This is not an advisory role—this is a hands\-on leadership position responsible for building and standardizing a high\-performing, value\-based primary care model. You will work directly with the CEO and leadership team to drive clinical excellence, improve provider performance, and implement scalable systems that produce measurable outcomes.

**Responsibilities**

* Lead clinical strategy aligned with organizational growth and value\-based care initiatives
* Develop, implement, and standardize clinical workflows, protocols, and care pathways
* Oversee and mentor nurse practitioners and physician assistants
* Establish provider performance expectations and accountability structures
* Drive improvements in quality metrics (HEDIS, STARS, RAF accuracy)
* Increase utilization of value\-based services (AWV, CCM, TCM)
* Support payer strategy, including securing and optimizing value\-based contracts
* Analyze KPIs and implement strategies to improve clinical and financial performance
* Optimize EHR workflows, documentation templates, and reporting systems
* Establish clinical governance processes, including provider meetings and case reviews
* Ensure compliance with regulatory and clinical standards

**Qualifications**

* MD or DO with active, unrestricted medical license (Texas preferred)
* Minimum 5–7 years of clinical leadership experience (beyond direct patient care)
* Proven experience developing and implementing clinical workflows and operational systems
* Experience supervising nurse practitioners and physician assistants
* Demonstrated track record of driving measurable clinical growth and performance
* Experience with value\-based care models, including Medicare Advantage
* Experience securing or managing value\-based contracts strongly preferred
* Strong understanding of primary care operations (AWV, CCM, TCM, risk adjustment)
* Experience in a startup or high\-growth environment preferred

**Compensation \& Incentives**

* Competitive hourly rate: $185–$200 based on experience
* Performance\-based incentives may be available based on:
* Quality metric improvement (HEDIS/STARS, RAF)
* Growth in value\-based revenue
* Provider productivity and operational efficiency
* Successful implementation of standardized workflows

**Authority \& Scope**

* Direct influence over clinical protocols, workflows, and provider performance
* Involvement in provider hiring, onboarding, and performance management
* Strategic input on clinical expansion and care model development

**Ideal Candidate**

* Builder mindset with strong execution ability
* Data\-driven and outcomes\-focused
* Comfortable working in a fast\-paced, entrepreneurial environment
* Hands\-on leader who can align clinical and operational priorities

**About ADIRA Health**

* ADIRA Health is a rapidly growing primary care organization in the Rio Grande Valley focused on delivering high\-quality, accessible care through a modern, value\-based model. We emphasize operational excellence, scalable systems, and provider performance to improve patient outcomes and drive sustainable growth.

Pay: $185\.00 \- $200\.00 per hour

Work Location: Hybrid remote in McAllen, TX 78504

title: Fractional Chief Medical Officer (CMO)
description:
**Company Overview**  

At Allia Health Group, the umbrella organization for Southend Pharmacy, Brello Health, and Woven, we don’t just follow industry trends—we redefine them. Our mission is to commoditize anti\-aging solutions, making them affordable and accessible to the average consumer—not just the wealthy. By offering customized and cost\-effective wellness products that follow cost\-containment models, we aim to improve people’s quality of life and meet them wherever they are on their health journey.

**Job Summary**  

  

Allia Health Group is building a digital health system across men’s health, women’s health, and longevity. Clinical services are delivered through Allia Care, a national provider network supported by centralized clinical operations and an in\-house EHR (targeting Q3 2026\).


Allia Care powers clinical services for group subsidiaries and provides medical governance across the portfolio, including Brello Health, HelloWellness, and Southend Pharmacy.


We are hiring a remote Fractional Chief Medical Officer to be the senior\-most medical leader accountable for clinical decision\-making, clinical operations, quality \& patient safety, and healthcare information systems across the ecosystem, scaling reliably at 15,000\+ patients/month while maintaining a high bar for clinical integrity and compliance.

 **Key Responsibilities**

* Set the clinical vision and standards of care across men’s health, women’s health, and longevity
* Establish clear clinical decision rights and governance mechanisms across Allia Care and subsidiaries
* Own the clinical operating system including coverage models, clinician supply/demand planning, and operational KPIs
* Design safety\-first throughput guardrails to maintain clinical quality during growth
* Build and run a telehealth\-native Quality \& Patient Safety program
* Build and lead a high\-performing clinician organization including recruiting, training, and supervision
* Serve as executive owner of clinical product strategy for the in\-house EHR and clinical tooling
* Own clinical due diligence for new programs and treatments
* Own the clinical compliance posture in partnership with Legal and Compliance
* Maintain safe prescriber\-to\-pharmacy workflows for medications fulfilled through the pharmacy subsidiary

 **What We Require**

* MD or DO from an accredited institution with an active, unrestricted U.S. medical license
* Board certified in Internal Medicine, Family Medicine, OB/GYN, Urology, or related specialty
* 10\+ years of clinical experience, including 5\+ years in senior clinical leadership (e.g., Medical Director, VP Medical Affairs, CMO)
* Proven experience leading clinical operations, quality, and patient safety programs in a telehealth or tech\-enabled care environment
* Experience managing provider performance, peer review, and clinical governance frameworks
* Strong knowledge of U.S. healthcare regulations (HIPAA, state medical boards, telehealth, prescribing compliance)
* Demonstrated ability to partner cross\-functionally with Product, Engineering, Operations, and Compliance teams
* Experience working with EHR systems and clinical technology platforms  

(e.g., Epic, Athenahealth, eClinicalWorks, or proprietary systems)
* Experience with telehealth platforms and virtual care delivery models

**Preferred Requirement**

* Prior experience as a Chief Medical Officer or equivalent in a digital health or multi\-entity organization
* Experience scaling clinical operations in high\-volume environments (10K\+ patients/month)
* Experience building or influencing EHR systems, clinical workflows, and decision support tools
* Experience managing multi\-state provider networks, including 1099 clinicians
* Familiarity with pharmacy\-integrated care models, including medication safety and 503A environments
* Experience with healthcare analytics and reporting tools  

(e.g., Tableau, Power BI, Looker)

**What We Offer**

* Full benefits package including medical, vision, dental, 401(k) with company match, PTO, Flex days, holidays, and more!

**Allia Health Group** *does not provide employment visa sponsorship now or in the future. Applicants must be legally authorized to work in the United States without the need for current or future sponsorship.*

**Equal Opportunity Employer Statement**  

**Allia Health Group** *is proud to be an Equal Opportunity Employer where we are committed to fostering a diverse and inclusive workplace. We are committed to cultivating a culture where all team members feel valued \& respected. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex, gender identity or expression, sexual orientation, national origin, genetic information, disability, age, veteran status, or any other characteristics protected by applicable law.*  

  

*If you have any questions or require immediate assistance or accommodations during the application or interview process, please contact us at recruiting@alliahealth.co.*

title: Director of Growth Marketing
description:
Description:
**About True North**
--------------------


True North is a leading innovator in Marketing and Advertising for healthcare. Our mission is to be the premier partner for healthcare leaders seeking data\-informed marketing strategies rooted in true consumer insights that drive demonstrable growth and well\-being for our clients and the communities they serve. We are a team of passionate, creative, and driven individuals whose vision is to redefine healthcare marketing partnerships.


We see a future where healthcare leaders, with access to data\-driven insights, drive true value creation for their organizations. We aim to become the most trusted partner in shaping a future where marketing drives true health and wellness impact.

**The Role**
------------


The Director of Growth Marketing at True North will serve as a strategic marketing leader, responsible for defining and guiding the company's comprehensive marketing strategy. This role is focused on building True North’s brand equity and developing scalable new lead generation strategies. This individual will be an experienced, strategic thinker with a proven track record of setting up successful marketing strategies and leading B2B or ABM initiatives. You will act as the marketing champion, helping to shape True North's strategic narrative and positioning across all channels and sales enablement.

**Key Responsibilities**
------------------------

* **Marketing Strategy:** Define, own, and continually refine the company's long\-term marketing strategy and roadmap, ensuring alignment with C\-suite business objectives and sales targets.
* **Brand \& Strategic Alignment:** Work collaboratively with the CEO to continually shape True North brand, supporting the setting of strategic direction for consistent messaging, tone, and visual identity across all platforms and client touchpoints.
* **Digital Strategy Oversight \& Execution:** Provide oversight and strategic direction for all digital marketing channels, including SEO/SEM, email marketing, social media, and content strategy to maximize brand visibility and lead generation architecture. Ability to set paid media plans and budgets, and navigate in platform execution.
* **Campaign Strategy:** Lead the strategic vision for innovative and effective marketing campaigns across multiple channels, with a focus on achieving measurable business outcomes and optimizing conversion paths.
* **Data \& Analytics Strategy:** Champion a data\-driven culture by advising on the utilization of data analytics, market research, and customer insights to measure performance, inform strategy, and identify growth opportunities. Oversee the reporting of key performance indicators (KPIs) to the executive team.
* **Collaboration:** Drive rigorous alignment between Marketing and Sales on external\-facing communication, leading the standardization of core sales enablement materials, including proposal decks, and capabilities overviews.
* **Market Intelligence \& Partnerships:** Stay ahead of industry trends, the competitive landscape, and emerging marketing technologies. Identify and negotiate strategic co\-branded marketing opportunities and potential referral pathways with adjacent healthcare tool and solution providers.
* **Conference and Tradeshow Strategy:** Plan True North’s tradeshow footprint, and manage speaking submissions and networking that are necessary to build the agency's presence at key industry events.
* **CRM Strategy:** Advise sales leadership on leveraging and optimizing the Hubspot CRM for marketing impact reporting and supporting the strategic evolution of business reporting needs.
* **Content Curation and Creation:** Excellent ability to guide creative partners in development marketing collateral, and ability to write content as needed for thought leadership, paid advertising, blogs, and potentially other needs.


Requirements:
**Qualifications \& Skills**
----------------------------

* Bachelor’s degree in Marketing, Business Administration, or a related field. 8\+ years of progressive experience in marketing, with at least 5 years of senior\-level experience with a focus on B2B marketing within the healthcare vertical.
* Proven success in developing and leading strategic marketing functions that resulted in significant business growth, ideally in a B2B or agency environment.
* Strong expertise in the full\-funnel of marketing, from brand strategy to digital execution, with a focus on scalable systems.
* Demonstrated experience in a consulting or fractional capacity, providing high\-level strategic advisory and mentorship.
* Exceptional analytical skills with a talent for interpreting complex business data and translating insights into clear, actionable executive strategies.
* Exceptional communication, presentation, and negotiation skills, capable of influencing C\-suite stakeholders.
* A strategic thought leader with a passion for brand storytelling and a bias for action and results.
* Proficient in leveraging marketing automation and CRM software for strategic oversight (HubSpot preferred).

**What We Offer**
-----------------

* The opportunity to make a significant, executive\-level impact on a fast\-growing, purpose\-driven brand.
* Flexible work schedules and 100% remote\-work environment.
* Engagement with a vibrant and dedicated team.
* Competitive health and welfare benefits including Medical, dental, vision, supplemental life insurance and discretionary 401k company match.
* Unlimited PTO and sick time.

title: Fractional General Counsel
description:
**About Everything But The House (EBTH)**


Everything But The House (EBTH) is a leading online marketplace for estate sales and unique second\-hand goods. We connect sellers and buyers across the country through curated online auctions, providing a seamless experience that combines technology, logistics, and expertise in valuation and resale.


As we continue to grow and evolve our marketplace, we are seeking an experienced Fractional General Counsel to provide practical legal guidance across the organization while supporting leadership with strategic legal oversight.

**Position Overview**


The Fractional General Counsel will serve as the company’s primary legal advisor on a part\-time basis (10 \- 20 hours per week, or as needed). This role will partner closely with executive leadership and cross\-functional teams to manage legal risk, support business initiatives, and oversee key legal matters, including contracts, employment law, compliance, and marketplace operations.


EBTH operates in a complex intersection of auction law, consumer protection, and multi\-state logistics, E\-commerce/Marketplace experience is a must.


This position is ideal for a seasoned attorney who enjoys working with growth\-stage companies and providing pragmatic, business\-oriented legal guidance and risk assessment.

**Key Responsibilities**

**Corporate \& Strategic Counsel**

* First point of contact for the internal EBTH team, responsible for resolving 80% of legal queries internally without the assistance of outside counsel. This will be aligned with a KPI to reduce outside counsel spend.
* Advise executive leadership on legal strategy and risk management.
* Support corporate governance and owner/board\-related matters as needed.
* Provide legal perspective on strategic initiatives, partnerships, and business models.

**Commercial Contracts**

* Draft, review, and negotiate contracts, including:
	+ Vendor agreements
	+ logistics and transportation contracts
	+ technology and SaaS agreements
	+ marketing and partnership agreements
	+ consignment and seller agreements

**Marketplace \& Regulatory Compliance**

* Ensure compliance with applicable federal and state laws related to e\-commerce, auctions, consumer protection, and resale.
* Advise on privacy, data protection, and digital commerce regulations.

**Employment \& HR Legal Support**

* Provide counsel on employment law matters, including policies, employee relations, investigations, and risk mitigation.
* Review employment\-related agreements and internal policies.

**Risk Management \& Dispute Resolution**

* Manage pre\-litigation matters and coordinate with outside counsel when necessary.
* Provide guidance on claims, disputes, and regulatory inquiries.

**Qualifications**

* JD from an accredited law school and active bar membership in good standing.
* 10\+ years of legal experience, ideally including in\-house counsel experience.
* Experience advising ecommerce, marketplace, logistics, or retail businesses preferred.
* Strong background in commercial contracts and employment law.
* Demonstrated ability to provide practical, business\-focused legal advice.

**Required Experience**

* Prior General Counsel or Fractional GC experience.
* Experience with multi\-state operations a plus.
* Experience with strategic partnerships, franchise law, and licensing agreements.
* Familiarity with auction, consignment, resale, or marketplace business models.
* Experience with IP \& Platform Protection. Protect "Partner Mode" software and proprietary intake algorithms when utilized by third\-party logistics operators.
* Experience working with executive leadership teams.

**Engagement Structure**

* Fractional engagement estimated at 10–20 hours per week.
* Flexible schedule with availability for executive leadership meetings.
* Ability to scale hours as business needs evolve.


We are not providing relocation for this position and are only accepting applications for candidates who are located in Ohio and/or the Cincinnati, OH metropolitan area.


We are an equal opportunity employer with a commitment to diversity. All qualified individuals are encouraged to apply.


Applicants must be authorized to work lawfully in the United States for EBTH. EBTH does not provide sponsorship at this time.

title: Marketing Manager
description:
**Position Title:** Marketing Manager


**Location:** Remote


**Employment Type:** Full\-Time (30\-40 hours/week), Flexible Schedule


**Compensation:** $27–$30/hour depending on experience


**Start Date:** Immediate

 **About Au Pair Weekend**



Since 2006, Au Pair Weekend has been dedicated to providing quality, affordable college courses for au pairs working in the United States. 2026 marks our 20th year in business! We've served over 60,000 au pairs from more than 90 countries. Our mission is to facilitate transformative educational experiences for au pairs and to strengthen the global community by connecting people, cultivating personal development, and maintaining excellence in all we do. For more information, visit: www.aupairclasses.org

 **Position Summary**



We are seeking an experienced, proactive marketing professional to fully own our marketing function and take APW's brand to the next level. As Marketing Manager, you will have responsibility for all aspects of marketing—digital advertising, brand, content, social media, email, partnerships, and community building.

  

You will report to the Director of Business Operations and receive regular strategic guidance from both the DBO and the Executive Director. Day\-to\-day, you'll be largely self\-managed, directing and managing content creation from staff and student contributors who generate UGC for APW's channels. This role is ideal for someone who takes initiative and feels comfortable taking ownership, not just executing a playbook.

 **Key Responsibilities**

 **Brand \& Strategy**


* Manage the full scope of APW's marketing activities under the strategic direction of the Director of Business Operations and Executive Director.
* Develop \- with guidance from the DBO and ED \- and execute annual marketing plans aligned with the organization's goals and OKRs.
* Lead weekly marketing / social media team meetings.
* Manage branded swag and event materials (design, ordering, inventory, shipping to event locations).
* Serve as final quality control on all public\-facing marketing content before publishing.
* Help develop and deliver on semesterly OKRs.

  


**Digital \& Performance Marketing**


* Manage and develop paid advertising (Google Ads, Meta Ads).
* Own SEO strategy and website design/optimization (Wix platform).
* Build and improve conversion funnels from awareness to enrollment.
* Oversee email marketing and SMS campaigns (HubSpot).
* Create and manage marketing automations and workflows.
* Own weekly/monthly marketing performance reporting and present insights to leadership.

  


**Content \& Social**


* Lead content strategy across Instagram, TikTok, YouTube, and emerging platforms.
* Build APW's presence on platforms beyond Instagram (TikTok, YouTube).
* Own blog content strategy and SEO\-targeted post calendar (currently 1\-2 posts/week).
* Create graphics for social media, email, and promotional materials (Canva or Figma).
* Ensure visual consistency and brand quality across all marketing assets.
* Manage: website design, content, updates (Wix).
* Create or direct compelling content that resonates with international, ESL audiences.
* Organize and host live virtual events and workshops via Zoom.

  


**Partnerships \& Community**


* Build relationships with major accounts and influencers in the au pair/cultural exchange space.
* Develop collaboration posts and partnership opportunities.
* Lay groundwork for community management and outreach.
* Proactively identify opportunities to grow APW's reach and reputation.

  


**Team Coordination**


* Direct and manage content creation from staff and student contributors.
* Provide creative briefs, feedback, and quality control for UGC and in\-house content.
* Coordinate with fractional contributors who support marketing alongside other organizational roles.

  


**Qualifications**

 **Experience**


* 3\-5 years of marketing experience with increasing responsibility.
* Proven experience with paid digital advertising (Google Ads, Meta).
* Demonstrated experience in social media management, content creation, and digital marketing.
* Experience in education, travel, cultural exchange, or mission\-driven organizations is a plus.
* Experience marketing to international or ESL audiences is a plus.

  


**Technical Skills**


* **Paid Ads:** Strong experience with Google Ads and Meta Ads.
* **SEO:** Strong knowledge and ability to improve organic performance.
* **AI Tools:** Strong familiarity with current AI tools and how to leverage them for efficiency.
* **CRM:** Experience with marketing automation platforms (HubSpot strongly preferred).
* **Graphic Design:** Strong skills in Canva or similar—you'll create assets, not just review them.
* **Website:** Basic website management and design skills (Wix or similar).
* **Work Management:** Familiarity with Google Workspace, Monday.com, Google Analytics, and Google Tag Manager.
* **WhatsApp:** Comfortable using WhatsApp and WhatsApp Business Manager as a communication and community channel.

  


**Soft Skills**


* Self\-starter mentality—you take ownership, bring ideas, and figure things out.
* **Important**: Sharp eye for detail and quality control.
* Comfortable producing regular performance reports and translating data into recommendations.
* Ability to work independently and manage your own priorities.
* Proactive and adaptable, able to work across departments effectively.
* Experience building a TikTok or YouTube presence from the ground up is a plus.
* Bilingual or multilingual a plus.

  


**Cultural Fit**



At Au Pair Weekend, we are committed to fostering a collaborative and inclusive environment that reflects our mission to provide transformative educational experiences for au pairs. We seek candidates who embody the following cultural attributes:


* **Mission\-Driven:** A genuine passion for cultural exchange and education, aligning with our goal to strengthen the global community.
* **Collaborative Spirit:** An eagerness to work closely with cross\-functional teams, valuing diverse perspectives to achieve common objectives.
* **Adaptability:** Comfort in navigating a dynamic, remote work environment, demonstrating flexibility and resilience in the face of change.
* **Proactive Mindset:** A self\-starter attitude, taking initiative to identify opportunities for improvement and drive projects forward.
* **Commitment to Excellence:** A dedication to maintaining high standards in all tasks, ensuring quality and consistency in our marketing efforts.

  


**Probationary Period**


* **Duration:** The probationary period for this role is set at three months, commencing from your official start date. This duration allows both parties to evaluate compatibility and performance.
* **Performance Reviews:** Regular check\-ins will be scheduled throughout the probationary period to provide feedback, address any concerns, and support your integration into the team. A comprehensive performance review will be conducted at the end of this period.

  


**Work Schedule**


* 30 \- 40 hours per week; flexible schedule based on business needs.
* Fully remote position with virtual team meetings.

  


**Benefits**


* Flexible work hours.
* 12 days of PTO per calendar year (tiered depending on average hours worked).
* 8 paid holidays.
* 401 (k) retirement option after 1 year of service.
* Opportunities for upward mobility in the organization if strong potential is shown.
* Collaborative and inclusive work environment.

  


**Join Us**



If you're passionate about marketing and eager to contribute to a mission\-driven organization that values personal development and global connection, we'd love to hear from you.

  

Please send your resume, cover letter, and portfolio. Examples can include a campaign or project you're proud of, graphic or audio\-visual assets, website design, proof of successful paid ad campaigns—bonus points if it shows both strategic thinking and hands\-on execution.

title: Associate Principal, AI Transformation
description:
**About Fractional AI**


Fractional AI builds bespoke gen AI software to transform businesses. We work with PE\-backed businesses and Fortune 500 clients across industries.


Every C\-suite in the country is trying to bridge the gap between AI in theory and AI in practice. We close this gap with a world\-class engineering team that implements custom end\-to\-end AI solutions, and a world\-class business team that provides AI advisory services. We are building a rapidly growing business at the forefront of applied AI and work with some of the largest PE firms in the world.


This is a company for intellectually curious and team\-oriented individuals looking to make an impact from day 1\.

**About the role**


The fastest way to learn how AI will reshape businesses is to go inside and analyze dozens of them. As an Associate Principal, you will be the intellectual core of our AI due diligence practice: assessing disruption risk and value creation opportunities across industries, interviewing management teams, and presenting findings to PE clients. In a given quarter you’ll see inside 4\-5 companies and develop pattern recognition that no other role in the market can offer.


That exposure is the best preparation to become a Fractional AI Principal \- the person PE firms call first when they want AI advice for their portfolio companies. Between diligences, you’ll work alongside our Principals on live deals, proposals, and PE relationship building. Within 6\-12 months, you’ll be expected to start leading your own deal pursuits.


This is not a quota\-carrying role, but is designed to grow into one.

**What you'll do**

* **Lead diligences end\-to\-end.** You are 100% dedicated to each engagement while the Principal and AI Architect are splitting time across deals and accounts. That means you are the connective tissue: you keep the POV sharp, prep the team before every interview, and make sure the Principal walks into a client readout fully armed. You own the day\-to\-day across desk research, POV generation, interviews, management meetings, and deliverables
* **Support live deals and account management.** You will work alongside Principals on active sales processes: participating in discovery calls, shaping proposals, and helping manage existing customer relationships
* **Build your private equity network.** Through diligences and deal support, you will build relationships with investors and portfolio company executives
* **Be a high\-utility player.** As an early\-stage company, the work extends beyond diligence. You may support adjacent work streams like partnership development, conference strategy, or internal tooling and process improvement

**What you bring to the table**

* **AI\-native**: you are genuinely interested in AI and have a working understanding of what generative AI can and can't do. You don't need to be an engineer, but you need to be credible in a room with one
* **Strong analytical horsepower**: you can quickly ramp up on an unfamiliar industry, identify what matters, and form a defensible point of view. With experience, you will quickly pattern match new project context to previous ones to accelerate time\-to\-value for clients
* **Relationship\-building instinct:** you naturally build rapport with senior stakeholders and are energized (not drained) by client\-facing work. You don’t need a sales background, but need to want one
* **Excellent communication skills**, written and verbal: you can translate technical AI concepts into business language that resonates with PE deal teams
* **Comfort with ambiguity and high ownership**: the diligence window is tight (2\-4 weeks), and you'll need to drive the work forward without waiting to be told what to do
* **Low ego, high resourcefulness**: you are both a doer and a strategist
* **5\-8 years of experience** in strategy consulting and/or early stage tech sales experience. Bonus: private equity, investment banking

**Snapshot of your first 3 months**

* You have participated in 3\-5 diligences alongside our Principals and AI Architects, progressing from supporting role to execution lead
* You are independently driving POV generation, interview prep, and deliverable drafting on active engagements
* You have participated in live sales or account management conversations alongside a Principal, and are starting to understand how we scope, price, and position our work
* You've identified at least one area where you can improve how we operate (process, tooling, templates) and have started making it better

**Why you should (and shouldn't) join us**


We believe in transparency and know joining a company is a big decision. We're not the best fit for everyone and try to be clear on what we are and what we're not so you make the choice that's right for you.

*Why join Fractional?*

* **We are filling a massive gap in a massive market:** AI diligence is a new category, most PE firms haven't done it before, and there are very few firms offering it. You will help define what this looks like
* **We have real momentum and demand:** PE firms know our name and are calling us when they need help. Our revenue has already tripled year to date and our go\-forward targets are incredibly ambitious and attainable at the same time
* **A front\-row seat to the AI revolution:** In a given month you will assess 3\-5 companies across different industries and evaluate how AI will reshape each one. The pattern recognition you build will be world\-class
* **A clear growth path:** This role is designed to grow into a Principal. You will learn the full lifecycle: diligence delivery, sales, contracting, and client relationship management
