title: Associate Principal, AI Transformation
description:
**About Fractional AI**


Fractional AI builds bespoke gen AI software to transform businesses. We work with PE\-backed businesses and Fortune 500 clients across industries.


Every C\-suite in the country is trying to bridge the gap between AI in theory and AI in practice. We close this gap with a world\-class engineering team that implements custom end\-to\-end AI solutions, and a world\-class business team that provides AI advisory services. We are building a rapidly growing business at the forefront of applied AI and work with some of the largest PE firms in the world.


This is a company for intellectually curious and team\-oriented individuals looking to make an impact from day 1\.

**About the role**


The fastest way to learn how AI will reshape businesses is to go inside and analyze dozens of them. As an Associate Principal, you will be the intellectual core of our AI due diligence practice: assessing disruption risk and value creation opportunities across industries, interviewing management teams, and presenting findings to PE clients. In a given quarter you’ll see inside 4\-5 companies and develop pattern recognition that no other role in the market can offer.


That exposure is the best preparation to become a Fractional AI Principal \- the person PE firms call first when they want AI advice for their portfolio companies. Between diligences, you’ll work alongside our Principals on live deals, proposals, and PE relationship building. Within 6\-12 months, you’ll be expected to start leading your own deal pursuits.


This is not a quota\-carrying role, but is designed to grow into one.

**What you'll do**

* **Lead diligences end\-to\-end.** You are 100% dedicated to each engagement while the Principal and AI Architect are splitting time across deals and accounts. That means you are the connective tissue: you keep the POV sharp, prep the team before every interview, and make sure the Principal walks into a client readout fully armed. You own the day\-to\-day across desk research, POV generation, interviews, management meetings, and deliverables
* **Support live deals and account management.** You will work alongside Principals on active sales processes: participating in discovery calls, shaping proposals, and helping manage existing customer relationships
* **Build your private equity network.** Through diligences and deal support, you will build relationships with investors and portfolio company executives
* **Be a high\-utility player.** As an early\-stage company, the work extends beyond diligence. You may support adjacent work streams like partnership development, conference strategy, or internal tooling and process improvement

**What you bring to the table**

* **AI\-native**: you are genuinely interested in AI and have a working understanding of what generative AI can and can't do. You don't need to be an engineer, but you need to be credible in a room with one
* **Strong analytical horsepower**: you can quickly ramp up on an unfamiliar industry, identify what matters, and form a defensible point of view. With experience, you will quickly pattern match new project context to previous ones to accelerate time\-to\-value for clients
* **Relationship\-building instinct:** you naturally build rapport with senior stakeholders and are energized (not drained) by client\-facing work. You don’t need a sales background, but need to want one
* **Excellent communication skills**, written and verbal: you can translate technical AI concepts into business language that resonates with PE deal teams
* **Comfort with ambiguity and high ownership**: the diligence window is tight (2\-4 weeks), and you'll need to drive the work forward without waiting to be told what to do
* **Low ego, high resourcefulness**: you are both a doer and a strategist
* **5\-8 years of experience** in strategy consulting and/or early stage tech sales experience. Bonus: private equity, investment banking

**Snapshot of your first 3 months**

* You have participated in 3\-5 diligences alongside our Principals and AI Architects, progressing from supporting role to execution lead
* You are independently driving POV generation, interview prep, and deliverable drafting on active engagements
* You have participated in live sales or account management conversations alongside a Principal, and are starting to understand how we scope, price, and position our work
* You've identified at least one area where you can improve how we operate (process, tooling, templates) and have started making it better

**Why you should (and shouldn't) join us**


We believe in transparency and know joining a company is a big decision. We're not the best fit for everyone and try to be clear on what we are and what we're not so you make the choice that's right for you.

*Why join Fractional?*

* **We are filling a massive gap in a massive market:** AI diligence is a new category, most PE firms haven't done it before, and there are very few firms offering it. You will help define what this looks like
* **We have real momentum and demand:** PE firms know our name and are calling us when they need help. Our revenue has already tripled year to date and our go\-forward targets are incredibly ambitious and attainable at the same time
* **A front\-row seat to the AI revolution:** In a given month you will assess 3\-5 companies across different industries and evaluate how AI will reshape each one. The pattern recognition you build will be world\-class
* **A clear growth path:** This role is designed to grow into a Principal. You will learn the full lifecycle: diligence delivery, sales, contracting, and client relationship management

*Why shouldn't you join Fractional?*

* **We are unapologetically building an engineering\-first culture.** This is how we build the team to meet the moment. We overdeliver for our clients by crafting a culture that enables the best engineers to thrive
* **We are a professional services business.** You will spend your time with customers advising, not building a product
* **We are in\-person.** We're committed to building an in\-person team in San Francisco and New York. This enables us to learn faster together. For the right person, this is a pro, not a con, but it's non\-negotiable
* **We meet our clients where they are.** As in\-person collaboration is non\-negotiable internally, the same applies externally. You should expect to travel about 1\-2x a month

**Our Values**


We overdeliver.


We overuse AI.


We "over engineer" the culture.

San Francisco, CA

title: Revenue Cycle Management Consultant
description:
What We're Looking For

Required:

\- 5\+ years of hands\-on revenue cycle management experience in outpatient or primary care settings

\- Strong knowledge of E/M coding (99202\-99215\), CPT coding, ICD\-10, and modifier usage

\- Hands\-on CAQH, PECOS, and payer enrollment/credentialing experience

\- Proven ability to conduct denial root\-cause analysis and implement corrective workflows

\- Experience working in common EHR/PM systems (Athena, eClinicalWorks, NextGen, AdvancedMD, or similar)

\- Comfortable on client\-facing calls — you can present findings to a physician or practice administrator clearly and confidently

\- Self\-directed and able to work independently with minimal supervision

Preferred:

\- CPC, CPMA, or similar AAPC certification

\- Experience with rural health clinics (RHC) or FQHC billing

\- Prior consulting experience or experience managing a billing team

\- Experience training or supervising junior billers or offshore resources

\- Familiarity with KPI dashboards and revenue cycle reporting

Why This Role

\- Flexible, remote, part\-time — work on your own schedule

\- Ground\-floor opportunity to help build an RCM firm from scratch

\- Your hours and income grow as we add clients

\- Clear path to full\-time Director role with profit\-sharing

\- You'll be the \#2 in the company — not a cog in a machine

\- Work directly with the founder on every engagement

Compensation

\- $55 \- $75 per hour, 1099 contractor

\- 15\-20 hours per week to start

\- At 5\+ clients, expect 25\-30\+ hours per week ($5,500\-$9,000\+ per month)

\- Growth path to full\-time with profit\-sharing as company scales

How to Apply

Send your resume and a brief note about your RCM experience to: asim.ahmed@northfieldrcm.com

In your note, please include:

1\. How many years of outpatient/primary care RCM experience you have

2\. Which EHR/PM systems you've worked in

3\. Your experience with credentialing/payer enrollment

4\. Your hourly rate expectation

5\. Your availability (hours per week and general schedule)

We're moving quickly — qualified candidates will hear back within 48 hours.

About Northfield RCM

Northfield RCM is a revenue cycle management consulting firm serving independent primary care clinics and outpatient practices. We provide fractional RCM leadership, credentialing and payer enrollment support, denial management, and workflow optimization, helping clinics reduce revenue leakage without hiring a full\-time RCM director.

We are building our founding team and looking for an experienced revenue cycle professional to serve as our lead RCM Consultant.

The Role

You will be the clinical and operational backbone of our delivery. You'll lead RCM assessments for new clients, identify revenue leakage and denial root causes, manage credentialing and payer enrollment projects, and serve as fractional RCM leadership on ongoing retainer engagements. You'll join client\-facing calls and present findings alongside the founder.

This is a 1099 contractor role starting at 15\-20 hours per week with a flexible schedule. As we add clients, your hours and income grow. This has a clear path to becoming a full\-time Director of Revenue Cycle role with profit\-sharing potential.

What You'll Do

\- Lead RCM assessments: review claims data, denial patterns, AR aging, payer mix, credentialing status, and front\-end workflows for independent primary care clinics

\- Build 90\-day revenue recovery plans with prioritized findings, KPI baselines, and actionable recommendations

\- Manage credentialing and payer enrollment: CAQH profile maintenance, payer applications, enrollment status tracking, revalidation, and issue escalation

\- Conduct denial root\-cause analysis, design worklists, and lead denial recovery efforts

\- Provide fractional RCM leadership: weekly KPI review calls with clients, issue management, staff guidance, vendor coordination, and executive reporting

\- Join client\-facing discovery calls and help present assessment findings professionally

\- Help build reusable SOPs, templates, checklists, and dashboards that standardize our delivery

\- Over time, oversee offshore billing and credentialing resources as we scale

Pay: $65\.00 \- $100\.00 per hour

Expected hours: 15\.0 – 20\.0 per week

Benefits:

* Flexible schedule

Work Location: Remote

title: Counsel – Legal & Contracts
description:
Description:

OKSI seeks an experienced Counsel – Legal \& Contracts to serve as the company's primary internal legal resource for contracting, legal operations, and protection of intellectual property and data rights. This is a hands\-on, execution\-focused individual contributor role working closely with the Vice President of Operations, the Contracts Administrator, business stakeholders, and the fractional General Counsel. The attorney in this role is expected to independently manage routine and moderately complex legal and contracts matters across commercial and government programs, and to partner with the fractional General Counsel on novel, strategic, or high\-risk issues.


This position is best suited for an attorney who remains active in the day\-to\-day work of drafting, redlining, and negotiating commercial and government agreements.

 **What You'll Do**

* Draft, review, revise, and negotiate a broad range of agreements including NDAs, customer agreements, vendor and supplier contracts, subcontracts, teaming agreements, reseller/VAR/channel partner agreements, software and technology agreements, licensing agreements, statements of work, and amendments
* Serve as the primary internal reviewer for legal and commercial terms including indemnification, limitation of liability, IP ownership, licensing, confidentiality, data rights, payment terms, warranties, termination, and dispute resolution
* Independently manage agreements end\-to\-end, including proposing positions, evaluating tradeoffs, and determining when escalation is necessary
* Advise internal teams on legal and contractual matters arising under government prime contracts, subcontracts, solicitations, proposals, awards, and modifications
* Review and interpret FAR/DFARS flowdowns, representations and certifications, and related government\-contracting requirements, and provide practical guidance to program and business teams
* Structure and negotiate positions related to SBIR/STTR rights, technical data and software rights, deliverable markings, and subcontract risk allocation
* Ensure OKSI's IP, software, technical data, and related rights are appropriately protected in both commercial and government contracting contexts
* Develop and maintain templates, fallback language, clause libraries, and negotiation playbooks to address recurring issues
* Build and improve legal intake, triage, tracking, and escalation processes in coordination with the Contracts Administrator and leadership
* Translate legal risks into clear, practical guidance for executives, engineers, program managers, and business development personnel


Requirements:
**Qualifications**

* 7–12 years of legal experience with significant emphasis on contracts, commercial transactions, government contracting, or related in\-house legal roles — candidates significantly above or below this range are unlikely to be a fit
* Currently active as a primary drafting and negotiating attorney — this role is not a fit for candidates who have moved primarily into oversight, management, or GC\-level responsibilities
* Demonstrated experience independently drafting, revising, and negotiating commercial agreements end\-to\-end
* Strong understanding of risk allocation, contract structure, and practical business judgment
* Ability to identify legal issues quickly, prioritize them, and propose workable solutions rather than only flagging concerns
* Comfortable doing the work directly — reviewing redlines, owning negotiations, and managing matters without delegating to junior staff
* Ability to collaborate directly with business stakeholders and deliver practical, actionable legal guidance
* Professional maturity, discretion, and ability to operate with significant autonomy and responsibility

**Preferred Qualifications**

* In\-house counsel experience within growth\-stage, operational, or engineering\-focused organizations
* Experience in aerospace, defense, robotics, autonomous systems, sensors, advanced manufacturing, or comparable technology\-driven industries
* Experience with FAR/DFARS, subcontracting, and government contracting environments
* Experience addressing IP, data rights, technical data and software rights, deliverable markings, or related legal issues
* Experience developing and implementing templates, playbooks, fallback positions, or legal workflow systems
* Experience with CLM, document management, e\-signature, or similar legal operations platforms

**Education \& Licensure**

* Juris Doctor (J.D.) from an accredited law school required
* Active membership in good standing in at least one U.S. state bar required
* Eligibility to register or qualify as in\-house counsel in relevant jurisdictions preferred

title: Accounting Manager - Client Lead
description:
**Graphite Financial** is a growing boutique finance, accounting, tax, and strategy firm (www.graphitefinancial.com) built to help startup and high\-growth companies scale efficiently and sustainably. Graphite Financial is redefining the “finance department” for early\-stage companies and VC\-backed startups. We deliver high\-caliber accounting and finance leadership and support to entrepreneurs and management teams building scalable, high\-growth companies. Our clients are actively managing cash burn, measuring performance, reporting to investors, making capital allocation decisions, and preparing for fundraising. All of that takes expertise and time that founders don’t have and can’t afford to hire for. Graphite bridges the gap with flexible, fixed\-fee support packages that provide fractional leadership and operational support from CFOs, Controllers, FP\&A Managers, and Tax leaders.

  

Graphite is looking for an experienced and process\-driven Accounting Manager/Client Lead ready to step into a high\-impact leadership role, guiding the financial strategy for a portfolio of high\-growth startup clients. We are seeking a dynamic and strategic leader with a proven record of establishing strong financial controls and successfully scaling accounting operations. This is a hands\-on position that requires deep technical expertise, exceptional client relationship skills, and a passion for the fast\-paced world of startups.

 **What You'll Be Leading**

  

This role is the direct owner of the client relationship, often partnering directly with the CEOs and executive teams of innovative, rapidly scaling companies. You won't just oversee, you'll strategize, implement, and deliver the financial clarity essential for their growth.

* **Client Leadership**: Serve as the primary, high\-touch contact for clients, owning the relationship and providing strategic advice on high\-level accounting, managerial accounting, and financial activities.
* **Team Development**: Manage, coach, and mentor a team of Staff and Senior Accountants, providing detailed review of their work to ensure impeccable quality and accuracy in financial reporting.
* **Operational Excellence**: Oversee all accounting functions, including the end\-to\-end monthly close process, financial statement preparation, and ensuring the smooth execution of day\-to\-day transactional work.
* **Strategic Project Management**: Coordinate and lead all activities for critical ad hoc projects, including external audits, due diligence support, and complex technical accounting research.
* **Financial Insight**: Deliver accurate, timely, and actionable client financial reporting to drive key decision\-making. This includes sophisticated analysis of key financial metrics, trends, and variances.
* **Cross\-Functional Collaboration**: Partner closely with our internal Finance and Tax departments to ensure every client's financial need is met with an exceptional level of customer service.

**Who You Are**

  

You are a strategic operator with the drive to build robust financial infrastructure for the next generation of industry leaders across a diverse portfolio of startups.

* **Experience:** You bring approximately 8\+ years of relevant client facing accounting experience (public accounting or corporate accounting), with at least 2 years of experience managing a team.
* **Education**: A Bachelor's or Master's degree in Accounting is required. A CPA or MBA is highly preferred, but not mandatory.
* **Technical Expertise**: Expertise in Web3 (Crypto) accounting and/or Consumer Packaged Goods (CPG) accounting is a plus.
* **System Proficiency:** Significant experience with core GL platforms like QuickBooks Online, NetSuite, or Sage Intacct, plus exposure to modern, tech\-forward systems (e.g., Bill, Ramp, Stripe, Gusto, Rippling, Cin7, Brex).
* **Communication**: Possess strong communication skills with the rare ability to translate complex accounting concepts into clear, concise language for clients, executives, and non\-financial stakeholders.
* **Mindset:** You are a team player comfortable in a fast\-paced environment, possessing a high degree of professionalism, motivation, and a genuine passion for working with growing startup companies

**Comprehensive Benefits \& Perks:**

* **Health \& Wellness:** Competitive Medical, Dental, and Vision coverage. 100% of the employee's health insurance premium is covered by the company.
* **Financial Security:** Company\-paid Short\-Term and Long\-Term Disability insurance.
* **Work/Life Balance To Include:** Generous Unlimited Paid Time Off (PTO), 11 Paid Company Holidays, and comprehensive Paid Parental Leave.
* **Remote Work Setup:** Fully remote position with company\-provided technology equipment and a $250 technology stipend to support your ideal home office setup.
* **401k:** With a company match


Compensation for this role is highly competitive and will be determined based on the successful applicant's experience with special consideration given to proven **Web3 (Crypto) and CPG accounting expertise** as well as geographic location.

 **The pay range for this role is:**  

96,050 \- 113,000 USD per year(Remote (United States))

title: CFO Services Senior Manager
description:
**Your Passion. Your Purpose.**


If you’re here, you’re looking for something more. More opportunity, more impact, more purpose. At Rehmann, each and every one of our associates plays a pivotal role in the Firm’s success. When you join our team, you can count on exceptional support, encouragement, and guidance from your colleagues and from leadership.

  

No matter where you want to go in your career, Rehmann can help you get there. Whether you’re in the early stages of your professional journey or you’re further down your path, we’re focused on helping you achieve your goals – whatever they may be. When you join Rehmann, you are part of a culture that Puts People First and aims to help everyone reach their fullest potential. Let us show you all the ways we can Empower Your Purpose.

  

To learn more about Rehmann, visit: https://www.rehmann.com/careers/

 **Job Description:**

  

We are seeking a talented consultant to join our CFO Services team. Rehmann’s Business Solutions group combines executive level talent with the latest technology tools to provide industry\-specific financial guidance that drive and maximize bottom\-line results. Our professionals provide our clients with flexible, customized financial solutions that support their key strategic decisions and day\-to\-day operations. This role will focus on the following areas:

* Managing \& performing execution for CFO Services projects focused in the following areas:


	+ Leading fractional CFO support engagements driving improvement in areas such as:
	
	
		- Developing dynamic financial models to support strategic planning
		- Provide financial leadership and support with budgeting, acquisition and other key business processes
		- Creating cash management and forecasting tools
		- Working with management on key metrics and driving business improvement
		- Completing department assessments
		- Improving month\-end closing, reporting and analysis
	+ Leading CFO oriented projects in areas such as:
	
	
		- Performing product or service cost studies
		- Completing profitability by product or customer projects
		- Improving ERP software utilization
		- Supporting due diligence project for buy/sell side activities
		- Lead refinance or restructuring efforts
* Developing the sales pipeline and market for CFO services


	+ Work in the following areas to develop a sale pipeline:
	
	
		- Internally with the business development team
		- Externally with the business referral networks, community and industry organizations
		- Driving opportunities for CFO Services and related transactional accounting outsourcing

  

We Put People First in all that we do. Our associates are our greatest assets and we provide programs and benefits that encourage growth and development and align with their needs and goals. This includes benefits focused on physical and mental health, paid time off for volunteering and diversity\-related activities, flexible work arrangements, and more.


When you join Rehmann, you become part of a firm dedicated to helping Empower Your Purpose, whatever it may be.


Rehmann is an Equal Opportunity Employer.

title: Executive Assistant
description:

title: Director of People Operations
description:
**About Allocate**
------------------


Allocate is an intelligent infrastructure platform for private market investing, equipping wealth advisory firms and fund managers to seamlessly source, build, and manage high\-quality private portfolios. The platform leverages AI\-driven investment management, comprehensive administration, and personalized access to opportunities at reduced minimums, making private market investing more efficient, transparent, and responsible.


We operate at the intersection of private markets and wealth, partnering with sophisticated investors and fund managers. With the opening of our new Palo Alto Headquarters, we are investing deeply in building a best\-in\-class internal and external client experience—supported by a culture defined by extreme client service, relentless problem\-solving, humility, and a bias toward action. Success in this role will come from knowing where to focus, bringing practical structure to a fast\-moving environment, strengthening leadership decision making, and putting in place the HR practices that allow the company to grow and operate more effectively.

**About the Role**
------------------


Allocate is excited to hire a Director of People Operations \& Talent to build and lead the next chapter of our people strategy. This role is ideal for a proactive, resourceful, and high performance people leader who can operate independently, anticipate needs, and design a scalable HR foundation for a fast\-growing, high\-performance organization.


As Allocate’s Director of People Operations, you will be the architect and operator behind our full employee experience—from talent acquisition and onboarding to performance, development, and engagement. You’ll partner closely with our executive team and fractional people operations partners to build the systems, programs, and cultural infrastructure that enable every employee to thrive. This is a hands\-on, high\-impact leadership role where you will balance strategic thinking with operational excellence to shape how we work and grow together.

**What You’ll Be Doing**
------------------------

 **Building and leading our People function:**  

Own daily HR operations, establish foundational systems, and partner with leadership to operationalize our people strategy.

**Tactical Execution and Strategic Thinker:**  

As the only in\-house HR person, you must be able to balance time between tactical execution of day to day HR Operations and also be a strategic thinker and implement overarching Company HR strategy.

**Partnering with leaders:**  

Help shape organizational design, anticipate hiring needs, and support managers in building healthy, high\-performing teams.

**Designing the full employee experience:**  

Create onboarding programs, define clear expectations, and build programs that support employees from pre\-hire to offboarding.

**Performance management \& engagement:**  

Oversee and manage, talent reviews (including 9\-box), feedback processes, and engagement programs informed by employee insights.

**Compensation, leveling \& benefits:**  

Review titles and pay practices, build a leveling framework, and lead compensation/equity benchmarking. Manage benefits, vendors, and evaluate HRIS/PEO solutions (including a potential transition from TriNet).

**HR compliance, policies \& employee relations:**  

Own handbook updates, manager guidelines, investigations, multi\-state compliance, and sensitive employee relations issues.

**Standing up scalable systems \& analytics:**  

Implement HR tools, documentation, dashboards, and reporting (headcount, attrition, recruiting metrics, compensation, etc.). Implement new HRIS to move off current system. Stand up and implement PEO for international hires.

**Driving hiring, recruiting operations, and workforce planning:**  

Partner with external resources to build structured hiring processes, job templates, compensation benchmarks, and a consistent candidate experience. Support managers through each step of the hiring lifecycle.

**Qualifications**
------------------

**Required:**

* 7\+ years of People/HR experience in high\-growth tech, finance, or fintech environments
* Experience building or scaling a People function from early to growth stage
* Expertise across core HR areas: talent, performance, compensation, benefits, compliance, and employee relations
* Strong command of HRIS, ATS, and people analytics tools with specific experience owning PEO transitions (Rippling, Justworks, etc.)
* Exceptional communication and relationship\-building skills
* A people\-first, high\-judgment approach to decision\-making
* A builder’s mindset—you enjoy creating clarity, structure, and systems in fast\-moving environments

**Nice to Have:**

* SHRM\-CP, SHRM\-SCP, PHR, or SPHR certifications

**Why Join Us**
---------------

* **High impact from day one** — You’ll build the infrastructure that powers Allocate’s next stage of growth.
* **Trusted leadership team** — You’ll work closely with leaders who value clarity, transparency, and operational excellence.
* **Strong product\-market fit** — Allocate is scaling quickly with strong demand across private wealth.
* **Team camaraderie** — We operate with humility, extreme client service, and a bias toward stepping in to help one another.
* **A beautiful new HQ** — Our Palo Alto office is designed for collaboration, focus, and a warm, high\-performance culture.

**Additional Details**
----------------------

* **Location**: Palo Alto, CA. **Must be able to work in the office 4 days a week.** We believe in the power of in\-person collaboration, fast problem\-solving, and a culture rooted in partnership, trust, and delivering exceptional service to our clients. We welcome applicants already in—or willing to relocate to—the Bay Area. (no company relocation allowance).
* **Employment**: Full\-time.
* **Salary Range**: $175,000 \- $215,000 base salary, plus performance\-based bonus, stock options, and comprehensive benefits.


	+ Actual salary will be determined based on the candidate’s experience, location, skills, training, certifications and education, other relevant qualifications specific to the role, and internal equity and market data. Total compensation may include equity and discretionary performance\-based bonuses based on individual, team, and company performance factors.
* **Benefits**: Medical, dental, vision, responsible time off (RTO), 401k.
* In compliance with federal law, all persons hired will be required to verify identity and eligibility to work in the United States and to complete the required employment eligibility


Compensation Range: $170K \- $200K

title: Finance / Controller
description:
**About Us**

Springy is a venture\-backed e\-commerce company and the exclusive vintage sportswear partner to Fanatics, the world’s largest sports merchandising platform. We curate, authenticate, and sell vintage sports apparel through Fanatics’ marketplace, TikTok, and our own direct\-to\-consumer channels.

We are looking for a seasoned fractional Controller or finance director\-level professional to own our financial operations, bring structure to our books, and serve as a strategic partner to leadership on budgeting, forecasting, and cash flow management.

**The Role**

This is a two\-phase engagement designed for someone who can first build the financial foundation and then maintain it on an ongoing basis.

Phase 1: Onboarding \& Build\-Out

During the initial engagement period, you will assess the current state of our financials and build the tools and processes we need to operate with clarity and discipline. This includes:

* Full review and cleanup of accounts payable, accounts receivable, and general ledger
* Establish chart of accounts and reporting structure appropriate for a venture\-backed e\-commerce business
* Build monthly financial reporting package (P\&L, balance sheet, cash flow statement)
* Develop a 12\-month budget and rolling forecast model
* Set up cash flow tracking and projections
* Review and organize existing vendor contracts, subscriptions, and recurring expenses
* Identify and resolve any outstanding tax, compliance, or bookkeeping gaps
* Establish internal controls and approval workflows for spending

Phase 2: Monthly Maintenance \& Strategic Support

Once the foundation is in place, you will transition to a monthly retainer covering ongoing financial management:

* Monthly close process: reconciliations, journal entries, and financial statement preparation
* Monthly budget vs. actual review with variance analysis and commentary
* Cash flow forecasting and runway monitoring
* Monthly budget/forecast meetings with leadership team
* Vendor and bill management: ensuring timely payments and accurate expense tracking
* Support on ad hoc financial analysis, investor reporting, and board materials as needed
* Annual closing of the books and coordination with external tax preparers/auditors
* Ongoing optimization of financial processes and systems

**Who You Are**

* 7\+ years of experience in accounting, finance, or controllership: with meaningful time spent in e\-commerce, DTC, or consumer brands
* CPA preferred; MBA or CFA a plus but not required
* Hands\-on experience with QuickBooks, Xero, or similar accounting platforms
* Comfortable with e\-commerce tools and data sources (Shopify, payment processors, marketplace platforms)
* Strong understanding of revenue recognition, inventory accounting, and cost of goods sold in an e\-commerce context
* Experience working with venture\-backed startups: you understand burn rate, runway, and investor reporting
* Able to build financial models from scratch and communicate findings clearly to non\-finance stakeholders
* Detail\-oriented and process\-driven, but flexible enough to work in a fast\-moving startup environment
* Comfortable operating independently with minimal oversight
* Experience with multi\-channel retail (marketplace \+ DTC \+ wholesale) is a strong plus

**What We Offer**

* Flexible, part\-time engagement with a venture\-backed brand at a pivotal growth stage
* Direct access to the CEO and executive team: your work will shape real business decisions
* Opportunity to build financial operations from the ground up
* A partnership with Fanatics, the largest player in sports retail
* Competitive compensation commensurate with experience

**To Apply**

Send your resume along with a brief description of your relevant experience, particularly any work with e\-commerce or venture\-backed companies. If you have examples of financial reporting packages, dashboards, or models you’ve built (anonymized is fine), we’d love to see them.

Pay: $2,000\.00 \- $6,000\.00 per month

Work Location: Remote

title: Sales Manager - Financial Services
description:
We are seeking a strong Senior Sales Executive for a Fractional CFO and Financial Services Firm. This industry and sales rely entirely upon relationships and trust. You will be responsible for driving business growth through networking, targeted cold calling, managing referral sources, and capitalizing on marketing leads.


Qualified candidates must have a background in value add selling, strong relationship development skills, be able to follow the company sales model, and take full ownership of sales for the company.


Responsibilities:

* Develop and implement effective sales strategies to drive revenue growth
* Identify and target new business opportunities in the market
* Build and maintain strong relationships with clients to ensure customer satisfaction
* Utilize the sales tools to track sales activities, manage leads, and analyze data
* Conduct outside sales visits to engage with potential clients and promote products/services
* Negotiate contracts and agreements with clients to close deals successfully


Skills:

* Proven experience in value add sales(financial background not required)
* Strong ability to analyze market trends and customer needs
* Excellent interpersonal skills with a focus on relationship development
* Proficiency in basic computer skills
* Demonstrated success in outside sales and business development
* Solid understanding of technical sales processes and strategies


If you are a motivated candidate looking to drive sales and take ownership, we invite you to apply.


\#Workwolf
